Content: Bailey: Directed towards, yeah.
Content_zh: Bailey：是朝那個方向，對。

Content: Well, it's complicated.
Content_zh: 嗯，這很複雜。

Content: Like that's, that is the question, that was my question for me and like, I mean a definite like, I'm definitely looking for a solution like this
Content_zh: 對，那就是問題，也是我自己一直在問的問題。我確實在找這樣的解法。

Content: in order to not have the game, you know, come out, you know, a year from now and, because I don't know where that money will come from
Content_zh: 這樣才不會讓遊戲拖到一年後才推出，因為我不知道那筆錢要從哪裡來。

Content: and what we'll have to do to get it.
Content_zh: 也不知道我們得做什麼才能拿到。

Content: [Music] Schafer: Anything else happen with Broken Age last week during GDC?
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：上週 GDC 期間，《Broken Age》還有發生什麼事嗎？

Content: Kipnis: Nice work hosting there.
Content_zh: Kipnis：你主持得很不錯。

Content: Schafer: Thank you, thank you.
Content_zh: Schafer：謝謝，謝謝。

Content: Thank you, thank you.
Content_zh: 謝謝，謝謝。

Content: Schafer: But what is beauty in a game?
Content_zh: Schafer：但遊戲中的美是什麼？

Content: Is it the most realistic depiction of a human?
Content_zh: 是最寫實的人類描繪嗎？

Content: Is it some sort of expressive stylized perspective on a reality?
Content_zh: 是某種對現實的風格化詮釋嗎？

Content: Is it, it is polygon count?
Content_zh: 是多邊形數量嗎？

Content: The answer is polygon count.
Content_zh: 答案就是多邊形數量。

Content: OK. Awesome. Well, great.
Content_zh: 好，太棒了。很好。

Content: Now that that is settled let's check out these nominees for the award for Best Visual Arts.
Content_zh: 既然這點解決了，我們來看看最佳視覺藝術獎的入圍作品。

Content: Schafer: I'm just glad that was over.
Content_zh: Schafer：我只是很高興那終於結束了。

Content: Petty: The Advisory Board, all, even people like Chris Hecker said you were really good.
Content_zh: Petty：顧問委員會的人，甚至像 Chris Hecker 這樣的人，都說你表現很好。

Content: Schafer: Even Chris Hecker?
Content_zh: Schafer：連 Chris Hecker 都這麼說？

Content: Kipnis: Even Chris, Schafer: He didn't say I was like two Cliffy B's taped together or anything like that?
Content_zh: Kipnis：連 Chris 都這麼說。Schafer：他沒說我像兩個 Cliffy B 被膠帶黏在一起之類的嗎？

Content: Chris Hecker joke. No.
Content_zh: Chris Hecker 的玩笑。沒有。

Content: That's a really insider thing.
Content_zh: 這真的是很圈內的梗。

Content: [Applause] Team Member: And then Greg doing work with Broken Age
Content_zh: [掌聲] 團隊成員：還有 Greg 做《Broken Age》的工作，

Content: and all the PR stuff around GDC, I think he especially liked seeing that trailer roll out.
Content_zh: 以及 GDC 前後所有的公關工作。我想他特別喜歡看到那支預告公開。

Content: It kind of gave me chills.
Content_zh: 那真的讓我起雞皮疙瘩。

Content: It was cool, Greg.
Content_zh: 很酷，Greg。

Content: Schafer: That was awesome.
Content_zh: Schafer：太棒了。

Content: And in our forums it was like, it was funny to see how many people were touched by the line about the 90,000 closest friends.
Content_zh: 在我們的論壇上，看到那麼多人被「九萬位最親密的朋友」那句打動，還滿有意思的。

Content: Like a lot of people were like, I was crying when I saw that.
Content_zh: 很多人都說：「我看到那段就哭了。」

Content: And I was like, oh man, i'm glad we tossed that in there.
Content_zh: 我就想，天啊，還好我們把那句放進去了。

Content: I didn't think... Bailey: Yeah, after the trailer's released, just watching the reaction of the crowd, it was pretty surreal.
Content_zh: 我沒想到……Bailey：對，預告公開後，光是看觀眾的反應就很不真實。

Content: And you know, I had the benefit of also, I think the viewers had the benefit of watching the reaction of the team, I think was also amazing.
Content_zh: 而且觀眾也能看到團隊的反應，我覺得那也很棒。

Content: So it's right in line with, you know, something big.
Content_zh: 所以這確實讓人覺得，我們正在做一件大事。

Content: I think it gave a lot of people chills and you know, I think we're onto something special here.
Content_zh: 我想那讓很多人起雞皮疙瘩，也讓人覺得我們真的做出特別的東西了。

Content: Rice: Yeah, we had a lot of stuff last month.
Content_zh: Rice：對，我們上個月真的忙了很多事。

Content: That seems like it was like five months ago, though.
Content_zh: 但感覺像是五個月前的事了。

Content: But yeah, it was good.
Content_zh: 不過對，還不錯。

Content: It was nice to be able to talk about it publicly, get the game out there a little bit more.
Content_zh: 能公開談這款遊戲、讓更多人看到它，感覺很好。

Content: People seem excited.
Content_zh: 大家看起來都很興奮。

Content: Schafer: Thanks for coming, everybody.
Content_zh: Schafer：謝謝大家來。

Content: And are there any backers of our project here?
Content_zh: 現場有我們這個專案的贊助者嗎？

Content: Backers? [Cheering].
Content_zh: 贊助者？[歡呼聲]

Content: Rice: I heard nothing but positive things.
Content_zh: Rice：我聽到的全都是正面反應。

Content: I even like tried to dig for bad things, like I always do, even though I like hate looking at certain message forums and message boards
Content_zh: 我甚至像平常一樣刻意去挖負面意見，雖然我很討厭看某些論壇和留言板，

Content: and comments in certain websites.
Content_zh: 還有某些網站上的留言。

Content: But like you do it just to see what people are saying and to know all aspects of what people are saying about the game.
Content_zh: 但你還是會去看，為了知道大家在說什麼，了解大家對遊戲的各種看法。

Content: And the places that usually people are pretty harsh, they all seemed pretty positive.
Content_zh: 平常大家比較嚴厲的地方，這次看起來也都挺正面。

Content: Schafer: And we're finally on the cover of Game Informer!
Content_zh: Schafer：我們終於登上 Game Informer 封面了！

Content: What? Batman?
Content_zh: 什麼？蝙蝠俠？

Content: Where is it, where is it?
Content_zh: 在哪裡，在哪裡？

Content: Oh, here it is.
Content_zh: 喔，找到了。

Content: Delivering on a Kickstarter promise.
Content_zh: 履行 Kickstarter 承諾。

Content: That's nice. Good.
Content_zh: 很好，不錯。

Content: Here, Oliver.
Content_zh: Oliver，給你。

Content: Here's one for you. Franzke: Thanks.
Content_zh: 這本給你。Franzke：謝謝。

Content: Schafer: Takes the cake.
Content_zh: Schafer：真是厲害。

Content: Head in the clouds.
Content_zh: 人在雲端。

Content: Here, want one of these?
Content_zh: 來，要拿一本嗎？

Content: Kipnis: What is it?
Content_zh: Kipnis：那是什麼？

Content: Schafer: It's our article, Game Informer.
Content_zh: Schafer：是我們的報導，Game Informer。

Content: Schafer: See if you can find it, see if you can find it.
Content_zh: Schafer：看能不能找到，看看能不能找到。

Content: There's a special article I wrote about you in there.
Content_zh: 裡面有一篇我寫你的特別報導。

Content: Schafer: Can I drop these off?
Content_zh: Schafer：我可以把這些放這裡嗎？

Content: Game Informer, Double Fine, yay, they came.
Content_zh: Game Informer、Double Fine，耶，他們寄來了。

Content: I heard you tearing through the script.
Content_zh: 我聽說你很快就把劇本錄完了。

Content: Petty: I was just, Schafer: One take Petty.
Content_zh: Petty：我只是……Schafer：一鏡到底 Petty。

Content: Petty: There were a few improvised lines about anal dildos, but no, there weren't.
Content_zh: Petty：我即興加了幾句肛門假陽具的台詞，不過沒有啦，其實沒有。

Content: Just kidding.
Content_zh: 開玩笑的。

Content: Schafer: Thanks for pointing that out, 'cause the camera's here.
Content_zh: Schafer：謝謝你指出這點，因為攝影機在這裡。

Content: Petty: Hey Paul! Whenever Paul's around I think of anal dildos.
Content_zh: Petty：嘿，Paul！每次 Paul 在旁邊，我就會想到肛門假陽具。

Content: Schafer: He's kidding.
Content_zh: Schafer：他在開玩笑。

Content: Those anal dildo lines are written in the script.
Content_zh: 那些肛門假陽具的台詞本來就寫在劇本裡。

Content: He's always taking credit for the best stuff.
Content_zh: 他老是把最好的東西都攬成自己的功勞。

Content: Schafer: You probably heard of the company's outrageously successful Kickstarter campaign.
Content_zh: Schafer：你可能聽過這家公司那個成功得離譜的 Kickstarter 募資專案。

Content: Offscreen Voice: Hey, he's walking through the whole intro.
Content_zh: 畫外音：嘿，他把整段開場都講出來了。

Content: Schafer: Surprise party's waiting for her.
Content_zh: Schafer：驚喜派對在等她。

Content: That's, it's called a "surprise" party.
Content_zh: 這就是所謂的「驚喜」派對。

Content: I said don't give too many spoilers out and he goes, he's like I get paid by the word.
Content_zh: 我說別爆太多劇透，結果他說他是按字數收錢的。

Content: Offscreen Voice: Is that what he said?
Content_zh: 畫外音：他是這麼說的嗎？

Content: Schafer: No.
Content_zh: Schafer：不。

Content: I'm like, we gotta fill this stuff with something.
Content_zh: 我就想，我們總得拿些東西來填滿這些篇幅。

Content: Double Fine still has work to do but if Broken Age stays the course, we'll all in for a treat.
Content_zh: Double Fine 還有很多工作要做，但如果《Broken Age》能照這樣走下去，我們就有好戲看了。

Content: I'd buy that.
Content_zh: 這個我買單。

Content: I'm going to buy that game.
Content_zh: 我要買那款遊戲。

Content: Oh, everything after that last, after that first announcement trailer and the game forum thing was all positive,
Content_zh: 喔，第一次公開預告和 Game Informer 那件事之後，所有反應都很正面。

Content: I think we got a lot of just excitement out there.
Content_zh: 我覺得外界真的很興奮。

Content: I stayed away from any mean forum comments or YouTube comments.
Content_zh: 我有避開那些惡意的論壇留言或 YouTube 留言。

Content: I didn't' see too many of those.
Content_zh: 我沒看到太多那種留言。

Content: It was all great.
Content_zh: 一切都很棒。

Content: It was all great, I think for people to see it.
Content_zh: 我覺得讓大家看到它，整體都很棒。

Content: Schafer: OK. Exciting.
Content_zh: Schafer：好，令人振奮。

Content: Now back to work.
Content_zh: 現在回去工作吧。

Content: And we're done with all that marketing stuff.
Content_zh: 那些行銷宣傳的事情都告一段落了。

Content: Now we're back to full-time working on the game content, which is exciting.
Content_zh: 現在可以全職回來做遊戲內容，這很令人期待。

Content: [Music] Offscreen Voice: Are you excited to play the game?
Content_zh: [音樂] 畫外音：你會期待玩到這款遊戲嗎？

Content: Schafer: I've seen sneak peeks from the office and I'm super-excited about some beautiful stuff that I saw, some fancy polished things.
Content_zh: Schafer：我在辦公室偷看過一些東西，看到那些漂亮又精緻的內容，讓我超期待。

Content: I cannot wait to play it.
Content_zh: 我等不及要玩了。

Content: Ryken: I mean we're getting more and more color every day.
Content_zh: Ryken：我是說，我們每天都有越來越多色彩。

Content: It's so fun to go from like that black and white one and then just like start, you know,
Content_zh: 從那種黑白版本開始，然後慢慢加東西，真的很好玩。

Content: doing a few things in Photoshop and then you do an export and it's like, whoosh.
Content_zh: 在 Photoshop 裡做幾個調整，匯出之後就像「咻」一下。

Content: And just like it starts feeling like a real, real vibe to the scene.
Content_zh: 場景就開始真的有那種氣氛了。

Content: Schafer: Look at that awesome ladder work.
Content_zh: Schafer：看那個超棒的梯子動作。

Content: Do that again.
Content_zh: 再來一次。

Content: It just seemed like a whole different game.
Content_zh: 感覺就像完全不同的遊戲。

Content: Schafer: Like everything was super polished, all the doors open, close.
Content_zh: Schafer：一切都超精緻，所有門都會開關。

Content: You go in through them and sound effects happen and the effects were all in.
Content_zh: 你走進去會有音效，特效也都放進去了。

Content: And it was amazing.
Content_zh: 太神奇了。

Content: Like it really was very heartening.
Content_zh: 真的很令人振奮。

Content: Like I got, I started to get excited about the final product of the game.
Content_zh: 我開始對遊戲最後完成的樣子感到興奮。

Content: Like I always get excited about the progress, but seeing that, I was like oh, man.
Content_zh: 我一直都會為進度感到興奮，但看到那個時，我真的覺得，天啊。

Content: When people actually see this and get this as final adventure game that they bought and paid, you know, paid money for
Content_zh: 等玩家真的看到這個，拿到他們花錢買下的最終冒險遊戲時，

Content: and are playing, they're going to love this.
Content_zh: 他們玩起來一定會喜歡。

Content: Which is great.
Content_zh: 這很棒。

Content: 'Cause up until then you're just happy like oh, his head doesn't pop off anymore.
Content_zh: 因為在那之前，你只會因為「喔，他的頭不會再彈掉了」而高興。

Content: Oh man, the pixels are on the screen, this is amazing.
Content_zh: 天啊，像素出現在螢幕上了，太神奇了。

Content: But there it's actually starting to look like a kind of fancy game.
Content_zh: 但現在它真的開始像一款精緻的遊戲了。

Content: It's looking all fancy.
Content_zh: 看起來好漂亮。

Content: Peck: Yeah. Actually the spaceship's coming along quite a bit.
Content_zh: Peck：對，其實太空船已經進展很多了。

Content: Pretty impressed with it every time.
Content_zh: 每次看都很佩服。

Content: It's gotten really far, really fast so it's been really fun.
Content_zh: 它很快就推進到很後面，所以做起來很有趣。

Content: Franzke: This is temporary still, but if we go into this room, it actually looks pretty cool.
Content_zh: Franzke：這還是暫時的，但如果我們走進這個房間，其實看起來很酷。

Content: So again, you have a sweet reflection on the ground and if you look at the door, obviously once it opens,
Content_zh: 你可以看到地板上很漂亮的倒影。再看門，當它打開時，

Content: actually that, like that reflects properly, too.
Content_zh: 那個也會正確反射。

Content: And you have like areas that, you know, are kind of more dirty now so they kind of block the reflection a bit more
Content_zh: 有些區域現在比較髒，所以會多擋掉一點反射。

Content: and there's like stuff that's completely opaque like a carpet.
Content_zh: 還有一些完全不透明的東西，像地毯。

Content: And that also blocks the reflections.
Content_zh: 那也會遮住反射。

Content: It works pretty well so I'm quite happy with how that turned out.
Content_zh: 效果很好，所以我對結果很滿意。

Content: And it' pretty cheap.
Content_zh: 而且效能成本很低。

Content: So you know, these days in games on a console, usually what you would do, you would basically, you render the entire image into a texture
Content_zh: 現在主機遊戲通常會把整個畫面算成一張貼圖，

Content: and then you can just like, basically use the texture to calculate those kind of reflection effects.
Content_zh: 再用那張貼圖去計算這類反射效果。

Content: On mobile devices doing that is really, really expensive, so we said we can't do it like that.
Content_zh: 但在行動裝置上這樣做成本非常高，所以我們說不能那樣做。

Content: So we're basically investigating different approaches of doing that.
Content_zh: 所以我們基本上在研究不同做法。

Content: And what we are doing now here is actually only, is using a super-old trick that very often we use during PlayStation One and PlayStation 2 times.
Content_zh: 我們現在用的其實是超老派的技巧，在 PlayStation 1 和 PlayStation 2 時代很常用。

Content: Where we take the geometry and we flip it and we render the geometry again.
Content_zh: 就是把幾何物件翻過來，再重新渲染一次。

Content: And it gives the impression like something is being reflected.
Content_zh: 它會讓人覺得好像有東西被反射了。

Content: So yeah, it's all just smoke and mirrors.
Content_zh: 所以對，這全都是障眼法。

Content: Smoke and mirrors.
Content_zh: 障眼法。

Content: Petty: So it lets us sort of get a variety of surfaces where you can sort of see like some of the light coming down here.
Content_zh: Petty：所以它讓我們可以做出不同表面的效果，像這裡你可以看到一些光灑下來。

Content: That's, I just painted that in.
Content_zh: 那是我直接畫進去的。

Content: It looks like the light was stretching out there.
Content_zh: 看起來像光從那邊延伸過來。

Content: It's all fake.
Content_zh: 全都是假的。

Content: You can see our lighting system in action here, too, actually.
Content_zh: 其實你也能在這裡看到我們的照明系統運作。

Content: Is that, you know, he kind of gets into this dark area and he gets darker, but I've been going through doing lighting on all these scenes
Content_zh: 你看，他走進比較暗的區域就會變暗。我一直在替這些場景打光，

Content: as we've been painting them, so he's gets a sort of orange, subtle orange rim light coming in off this area.
Content_zh: 我們畫這些場景時，我也一直在打光，所以這一區會投來一點微妙的橘色邊光。

Content: You can sort of see it, I can zoom in here a little bit.
Content_zh: 你可以看到，我可以放大一點這裡。

Content: And you can kind of see this subtle orange stuff here on his shoulders and that goes away as he walks away from the source.
Content_zh: 你可以看到他肩膀上有一點細微的橘色光，離開光源後就消失了。

Content: You can see here it's entirely gone.
Content_zh: 你可以看到它完全消失了。

Content: And just as a demonstration of how powerful this lighting is, this is what he looks like in the scene with no lighting.
Content_zh: 為了展示打光有多重要，這是場景完全沒有打光時他的樣子。

Content: So even the smallest amount of color and grading helps him sit in the scene.
Content_zh: 所以就算只是很少的色彩和調色，也能讓他更融入場景。

Content: And since we have such a diverse set of environments, people can go in, it was really important, you know.
Content_zh: 我們的環境變化很多，玩家又會走進去，所以這點真的很重要。

Content: I think lighting is one of those, it's always one of the best tools in any game, to sculpt the game play and influence the player in a non-heavy handed way.
Content_zh: 我覺得打光一直是遊戲裡最好的工具之一，可以塑造玩法，也能用不強硬的方式影響玩家。

Content: So it's actually a really powerful tool that I think a lot of people don't spend enough time with.
Content_zh: 所以它其實是很強大的工具，我覺得很多人花在上面的時間不夠。

Content: And lighting's always been one of my focuses, just because I, as an art director, it's one of the easiest things to go in later and both address game play issues
Content_zh: 打光一直是我很重視的事，因為身為美術總監，這是後期最容易進去調整的東西之一，既能處理玩法問題，

Content: but also just mood issues, you know, to kind of push and pull it, to get it closer to where the story's going.
Content_zh: 也能調整氣氛，讓它更接近故事要去的方向。

Content: Franzke: Lydia did all this sweet like teleporter effects, which just look pretty sweet.
Content_zh: Franzke：Lydia 做了這些很棒的傳送器特效，看起來真的很酷。

Content: Like every week, I'd look at the game like all the teleporter effect again awesome, just absolutely amazing.
Content_zh: 我每週看遊戲時，都會覺得傳送器特效又更棒了，真的很驚人。

Content: Choy: A lot of times for ambient effects people, like usually I'm the only person thinking about it,
Content_zh: Choy：很多時候，環境特效這塊通常只有我一個人在想，

Content: what can, how we can make the scene less static.
Content_zh: 就是要怎麼讓場景不要那麼靜態。

Content: So I think it usually surprises people, which is fun.
Content_zh: 所以我覺得它常常會讓大家驚喜，這很好玩。

Content: Yeah, so this one was cool because I asked the artists to, like they, they often paint in lighting.
Content_zh: 對，這個很酷，因為美術常常會把光直接畫進去，

Content: But I let, asked them to keep it on a separate layer in Photoshop so I could like break it out and animate it with effects.
Content_zh: 但我請他們在 Photoshop 裡把它放在獨立圖層，這樣我就能拆出來，加上特效動畫。

Content: Because before these were just like static and part of the scene layer.
Content_zh: 因為以前那些都只是靜態的，直接在場景圖層裡。

Content: Schmitz: This is 3D?
Content_zh: Schmitz：這是 3D 嗎？

Content: Crook: Yeah, so it's like, it's like, it's kind of fake-o.
Content_zh: Crook：對，所以它算是，有點假假的 3D。

Content: Why, why is it not?
Content_zh: 為什麼，為什麼不算？

Content: Schmitz: This is all red back here.
Content_zh: Schmitz：後面這裡全都是紅色。

Content: So basically like the whole UI is like these panels, there's like the settings.
Content_zh: 基本上整個 UI 都是這些面板，還有設定之類的東西。

Content: And you can like make levels and we did all these icons and stuff.
Content_zh: 你可以製作關卡，我們也做了這些圖示等等。

Content: It was like a lot of design stuff.
Content_zh: 有很多設計工作。

Content: It took forever.
Content_zh: 花了超久。

Content: Garner: Really, that's cool.
Content_zh: Garner：真的，那很酷。

Content: That is a hefty line job.
Content_zh: 這線條工程很紮實。

Content: Schmitz: Yeah, it was huge.
Content_zh: Schmitz：對，量很大。

Content: Rice: And Corey is kind of like a friend of a friend.
Content_zh: Rice：Corey 算是朋友的朋友。

Content: I've a lot of kind of mutual friends with him and I'd seen his stuff and I always liked his stuff
Content_zh: 我跟他有很多共同朋友，也看過他的作品，一直都很喜歡。

Content: and that it's really simple and kind of retro, but new.
Content_zh: 而且很簡單，有點復古，但又很新。

Content: And just really slick and clean.
Content_zh: 也很俐落、乾淨。

Content: And so it seemed like it would be a good fit and he'll be on the project for a month or two.
Content_zh: 所以感覺他會很適合，而且他會加入這個專案一兩個月。

Content: And kicking off recently we just had our first meeting with him and he'll be checking in some UI stuff.
Content_zh: 最近剛開始，我們才跟他開了第一次會，他會提交一些 UI 的東西。

Content: Schmitz: Yeah, I kind of like the handcrafted-ness of the game.
Content_zh: Schmitz：對，我滿喜歡這款遊戲的手作感。

Content: That's something that I would want to have present in the UI if it's possible, it'd be cool.
Content_zh: 如果可以，我希望 UI 也能有那種感覺，那會很酷。

Content: Petty: Yeah, and I think that's probably the biggest way to connect it with the other visuals without having,
Content_zh: Petty：對，我覺得那可能是讓它和其他視覺連在一起的最好方法，

Content: without like just, you know, oh, it's painted, too, you know.
Content_zh: 又不會只是變成「喔，它也是畫出來的」那樣。

Content: Rice: If it comes to the point where you have like a really strong vision of what you want your stuff to look like
Content_zh: Rice：如果你對自己的東西該長什麼樣子有很強烈的想法，

Content: and it doesn't fit as much with the stuff that exists.
Content_zh: 而它跟現有的東西沒那麼搭。

Content: Schafer: Nothing is untouchable, remember that.
Content_zh: Schafer：沒有什麼是不能動的，記住這點。

Content: Lee's going to bring that up again, but it's going to mean something different.
Content_zh: Lee 之後會再提到這句，但意思會不太一樣。

Content: Lee: That's more a Greg comment.
Content_zh: Lee：那比較像 Greg 會說的話。

Content: Schafer: That's more of a, I don't know.
Content_zh: Schafer：這更像是一個，我不知道。

Content: Rice: That's a Lee comment.
Content_zh: Rice：這是 Lee 會說的話。

Content: Rice: You mean Lee towards Greg.
Content_zh: Rice：你是說 Lee 對 Greg 說嗎？

Content: Schafer: Unsolicited backrubs. Double Fine Nights.
Content_zh: Schafer：沒人要求的背部按摩。Double Fine 之夜。

Content: Petty: I mean I think we made good progress.
Content_zh: Petty：我的意思是，我想我們取得了很大的進展。

Content: I don't think, I don't think you can be unhappy with the look that we have.
Content_zh: 我不覺得大家會對現在的視覺不滿意。

Content: I, of course, always want to take it further.
Content_zh: 當然，我總是想更進一步。

Content: I still think we're not pushing parallax and multi-planning enough, you know, but I think that we have everything we need for that.
Content_zh: 我還是覺得視差和多層景深還推得不夠，但我想我們已經有需要的素材了。

Content: It's really just a matter of getting in further and find the right place to do those.
Content_zh: 只是要再深入一點，找到適合做這些的地方。

Content: And then there's a lot, I think once we get real voice acting and see final animation, some of the great effects work Lydia's been doing
Content_zh: 還有很多東西。我想等真正的配音進來、看到最終動畫，以及 Lydia 做的那些很棒的特效後，

Content: You see all those things together, hopefully they'll, goes to where it needs to be.
Content_zh: 把這些放在一起看，希望它就會到達該有的水準。

Content: Schafer: OK. Are we going to get, are we going to get fog on his helmet?
Content_zh: Schafer：好，我們會在他的頭盔上加霧氣嗎？

Content: Is that a thing?
Content_zh: 會有那個嗎？

Content: Rice: Get what?
Content_zh: Rice：得到什麼？

Content: Schafer: When he's breathing, hah!
Content_zh: Schafer：就是他呼吸時，哈！

Content: Did we talk about that last time?
Content_zh: 我們上次談過嗎？

Content: I thought we talked about that, like getting steam on the inside of his helmet?
Content_zh: 我記得我們聊過，像是在他的頭盔內側加霧氣？

Content: Yeah, that'd be cool.
Content_zh: 是啊，那會很酷。

Content: And there's something missing with the irises, missing on his feet, is that?
Content_zh: 虹膜是不是少了什麼？腳上也少了東西，是嗎？

Content: Yeah. Offscreen Voice: Yeah.
Content_zh: 對。畫外音：對。

Content: Schafer: After this I guess they're working on the intro to the girl's world.
Content_zh: Schafer：接下來我猜他們在做女孩世界的開場。

Content: The girl's called Vella, by the way, yeah.
Content_zh: 順帶一提，那個女孩叫 Vella。

Content: Short for Veluria.
Content_zh: 是 Veluria 的簡稱。

Content: They have names, yeah.
Content_zh: 對，他們有名字了。

Content: Vella and Shay.
Content_zh: Vella 和 Shay。

Content: The girl's named Vella; it's short for Veluria.
Content_zh: 女孩叫 Vella，是 Veluria 的簡稱。

Content: Her sister's named Rocky, which is short for Raquel.
Content_zh: 她妹妹叫 Rocky，是 Raquel 的簡稱。

Content: I wrote all the dialogue for the intro to her world and named all her parents, stuff like that.
Content_zh: 我寫了她那個世界開場的所有對話，也替她爸媽之類的人取了名字。

Content: Ryken: That's going to be fun.
Content_zh: Ryken：那會很好玩。

Content: It's funny because it's like you work on the Cloud Colony for so long and you're like, clouds, clouds everywhere!
Content_zh: 很好笑，因為你在雲端聚落做了那麼久，就會覺得：到處都是雲！

Content: And it's fun at first, and then you're just like, I'm sick of clouds.
Content_zh: 一開始很好玩，後來就會覺得：我受夠雲了。

Content: And then like, you're like, yay!
Content_zh: 然後你會覺得，耶！

Content: And like it's like we're in space.
Content_zh: 就像我們在太空裡。

Content: And like we're in like enclosed like, like hard surfaced everything.
Content_zh: 而且我們在密閉空間裡，所有東西都是硬質表面。

Content: This is super-fun.
Content_zh: 太好玩了。

Content: And then you're like, oh man, we've been in space for a long time.
Content_zh: 然後你就會想，天啊，我們在太空待好久了。

Content: And then like, yeah, you kind of like do look forward to, 'cause you live in these little like, I mean,
Content_zh: 然後你就會開始期待，因為你等於住在這些小小的……我是說，

Content: I'm sitting at a computer screen but you're really like in these little enclosed areas,
Content_zh: 我雖然是坐在電腦螢幕前，但做那些區域時，感覺真的像待在小小的封閉空間裡，

Content: you know, while you're working on them.
Content_zh: 你在做它們的時候就是這樣。

Content: And you spend a lot of time with them, so just the idea of getting outside again is like kind of fun, you know.
Content_zh: 你會跟它們相處很久，所以光是想到又能出去，就會覺得有點好玩。

Content: Schafer: So here's the Vella enter dialogue.
Content_zh: Schafer：這是 Vella 的進場對話。

Content: Lately, because I, previously did not know how to use the new dialogue database.
Content_zh: 最近，因為我之前不知道如何使用新的對話資料庫。

Content: And it's always, I never had time to learn, I've just been like, OK, put all this dialogue in.
Content_zh: 一直以來我都沒時間學，只是一直說：好，把這些對話放進去。

Content: So I wrote the Vella enter dialogue.
Content_zh: 所以我寫了 Vella 的進場對話。

Content: Like these are all just cut scenes or chatter.
Content_zh: 這些都是過場動畫或閒聊。

Content: So this is just like her mom talking, or if a stage direction in that column.
Content_zh: 所以這裡就是她媽媽講話，或者那一欄裡會有舞台指示。

Content: I put dialogue here and the name of the character.
Content_zh: 我在這裡放對話和角色名字。

Content: And then this column on the left is only, only has stuff in it, interactive dialogues where she, so here is the dialogue with the grandpa.
Content_zh: 然後左邊這一欄只會在互動對話時有內容，像這裡就是跟爺爺的對話。

Content: I tag each dialogue choice, like with one dash ten for being in node one, line 10.
Content_zh: 我會標記每個對話選項，像 1-10 就代表節點一的第 10 行。

Content: And then I say whether that line is on or off when you start the dialogue.
Content_zh: 然後我會標明開始對話時，那一行是開啟還是關閉。

Content: And then at the end of it, it's automatically assumed that line's going to go away unless I say, "always on".
Content_zh: 對話結束後，系統會自動假設那行要消失，除非我標成「永遠開啟」。

Content: And then there's all those logic for turning on and off other dialogue choices.
Content_zh: 然後還有那些用來開關其他對話選項的邏輯。

Content: So the dialogue choices, this is almost like a little macro, like a little mockup programming language in a way,
Content_zh: 所以這些對話選項有點像小型巨集，某種程度上像簡化版程式語言。

Content: 'cause the programmers can take this and turn it into logic, too.
Content_zh: 因為程式員也能把它變成邏輯。

Content: Make the dialogue choices work.
Content_zh: 讓對話選項能正常運作。

Content: And also, when I'm writing I had to play music.
Content_zh: 而且，我寫東西時一定要放音樂。

Content: It usually doesn't have lyrics unless it's really old heavy metal or Led Zeppelin that I played a million times.
Content_zh: 通常不能有歌詞，除非是那種我聽過無數次的老重金屬或 Led Zeppelin。

Content: I can always go into my Black Sabbath folder.
Content_zh: 我隨時可以打開我的 Black Sabbath 資料夾。

Content: [Rock Music] It's good.
Content_zh: [搖滾音樂] 很好。

Content: So I wrote some lines.
Content_zh: 所以我寫了一些台詞。

Content: Including the line "Really, SPLARG?
Content_zh: 包括這句：「真的嗎，SPLARG？

Content: Is it my birthday or something?" That's going to be, probably the best line in the game.
Content_zh: 今天是我生日還是怎樣？」這大概會是遊戲裡最棒的一句台詞。

Content: Can't tell now but ten years from now you'll know.
Content_zh: 現在還看不出來，但十年後你就會知道。

Content: [Guitar Solo] Schafer: So much for that design.
Content_zh: [吉他獨奏] Schafer：那個設計就到此為止了。

Content: Rice: Part of this was that I wanted to talk about all the potential meetings that we are supposed to think about.
Content_zh: Rice：其中一部分是我想聊聊我們可能得安排的那些會議。

Content: Schafer: A meeting about meetings that we gotta to have.
Content_zh: Schafer：開一場討論要開哪些會的會。

Content: Rice: I have a list here of like these are all the meetings that Tim is supposed to have early this week.
Content_zh: Rice：我這裡有一份清單，都是 Tim 本週前半該開的會。

Content: Let's figure out if we can do that.
Content_zh: 我們想想能不能做到。

Content: Schafer: How 'bout none of them?
Content_zh: Schafer：一個都不要怎麼樣？

Content: Rice: And that's, that's kind of what I was going to say.
Content_zh: Rice：這就是我要說的。

Content: Is that, Schafer: I don't want to have any meetings.
Content_zh: Rice：就是……Schafer：我不想開任何會。

Content: Rice: I know. I don't either.
Content_zh: Rice：我知道，我也不想。

Content: Schafer: I'm supposed to be writing every morning, dialogue and then designing with John afternoons. Rice: Yeah.
Content_zh: Schafer：我應該每天早上寫對話，下午跟 John 做設計。Rice：對。

Content: Schafer: Look, it's John Swisshelm.
Content_zh: Schafer：看，是 John Swisshelm。

Content: John, what are you doing here at dailies?
Content_zh: John，你來每日檢視會做什麼？

Content: John is here for a week.
Content_zh: John 這週會在這裡。

Content: Swisshelm: I'm going to help Tim out this week and maybe longer after this.
Content_zh: Swisshelm：這週我會幫 Tim，也可能之後再多幫一陣子。

Content: I'm excited to just look at, I've been looking at all the docs, through all the docs, catching up.
Content_zh: 我很期待能看看這些東西。我一直在看所有文件，努力補進度。

Content: Maybe I can be a good set of fresh eyes on some of the stuff you guys have been head down on.
Content_zh: 也許我能用新鮮的眼光看看你們一直埋頭做的東西。

Content: This is interesting for me, too, because I was backer before I joined Double Fine, so it's awesome to be working on this project.
Content_zh: 這對我也很有意思，因為我加入 Double Fine 之前就是贊助者，所以能參與這個專案很棒。

Content: Schafer: Thank you for backing, thank you for your backing.
Content_zh: Schafer：謝謝你的贊助，謝謝你支持我們。

Content: Paul's going to check that right now, can you give me a verification on that?
Content_zh: Paul 現在要去查，你能幫我確認一下嗎？

Content: Don't bring up how much he paid, though.
Content_zh: 不過別提他付了多少錢。

Content: Swisshelm: Let's just say I got t-shirt and a poster.
Content_zh: Swisshelm：就說我有拿到 T 恤和海報吧。

Content: Schafer: Yo, very nice, very nice.
Content_zh: Schafer：唷，很好，很好。

Content: Swisshelm: I don't know where the poster is anymore.
Content_zh: Swisshelm：我已經不知道海報放哪了。

Content: Schafer: Well, I always, I always need someone to bounce ideas off of and talk to, you know.
Content_zh: Schafer：我總是需要有人可以丟想法、可以討論。

Content: So sometimes if, sometimes I take the whole team, brainstorm with them or just take a few people on the team.
Content_zh: 所以有時我會找整個團隊腦力激盪，有時只找團隊裡幾個人。

Content: But everyone's working so hard now that, 'cause sometimes just sitting in your office by yourself,
Content_zh: 但現在每個人都很忙，因為有時候一個人坐在辦公室裡，

Content: even with a notebook it's really hard to kind of get through the thoughts and ideas.
Content_zh: 即使有筆記本，也很難把腦中的想法整理出來。

Content: And we were talking through, so when everyone contributes and talking with John, we had a bunch of ideas.
Content_zh: 我們一邊聊一邊推，大家都有貢獻，跟 John 討論後冒出很多想法。

Content: So it's just, it's just more productive to brainstorm with someone else.
Content_zh: 所以跟別人一起腦力激盪會更有成效。

Content: Schafer: Well, welcome to the project, Mr. Swisshelm.
Content_zh: Schafer：好，歡迎加入這個專案，Swisshelm 先生。

Content: Just to prove how natural and I don't notice the cameras at all, let's shake hands like this.
Content_zh: 為了證明這有多自然、我完全沒注意到攝影機，我們就這樣握手吧。

Content: Natural.
Content_zh: 很自然。

Content: Swisshelm: Nice wave we got going on here.
Content_zh: Swisshelm：我們這邊揮手揮得不錯。

Content: Schafer: Yeah.
Content_zh: Schafer：是啊。

Content: Schafer: Great to have you.
Content_zh: Schafer：很高興你來。

Content: Swisshelm: Good to be here.
Content_zh: Swisshelm：很高興能來。

Content: Schafer: You like my bar fight joke?
Content_zh: Schafer：你喜歡我的酒吧打架玩笑嗎？

Content: I was giving my joke, the solution to every design question.
Content_zh: 我那個笑話是，每個設計問題的解法都是：

Content: It'd be like, you should have a bar fight.
Content_zh: 就像：你應該安排一場酒吧鬥毆。

Content: That worked in Uncharted II.
Content_zh: 《Uncharted II》就很有效。

Content: Did I mention, no, Uncharted III.
Content_zh: 我有說錯嗎？不，是《Uncharted III》。

Content: Swisshelm: Three, the beginning.
Content_zh: Swisshelm：三代，一開始。

Content: Swisshelm: I wasn't on two.
Content_zh: Swisshelm：我沒參與二代。

Content: What else have you designed besides the bar fight in Uncharted III?
Content_zh: 除了《Uncharted III》的酒吧鬥毆，你還設計過什麼？

Content: Swisshelm: I did a lot of stuff, just here and there.
Content_zh: Swisshelm：我做過很多東西，零零散散的。

Content: Schafer: At EA, what did you do, Dead Space?
Content_zh: Schafer：在 EA 的時候你做了什麼？《Dead Space》嗎？

Content: Swisshelm: I was on Dante's Inferno.
Content_zh: Swisshelm：我做過《Dante's Inferno》。

Content: Schafer: Dante's Inferno.
Content_zh: Schafer：《Dante's Inferno》。

Content: Swisshelm: And some other games that will never come out.
Content_zh: Swisshelm：還有一些永遠不會推出的遊戲。

Content: Schafer: Was it your idea to have babies that you kill?
Content_zh: Schafer：那些要殺掉的嬰兒是你的點子嗎？

Content: Swisshelm: It was not my idea.
Content_zh: Swisshelm：那不是我的點子。

Content: Most of that was not anybody, well, it just one of those ideas, we had to just deal with it, make it as cool as we could.
Content_zh: 多半不是誰的點子，就是冒出那樣的想法，我們只能處理它，盡量做得酷一點。

Content: Schafer: So blame on it Dante.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以怪 Dante 就對了。

Content: Swisshelm: Exactly.
Content_zh: Swisshelm：沒錯。

Content: Schafer: OK. So the girl's world.
Content_zh: Schafer：好，所以女孩的世界。

Content: I've got to come up with a name for this world.
Content_zh: 我得替這個世界想個名字。

Content: The Seedlins or something, is where all the, OK.
Content_zh: Seedlins 之類的，所有的……好。

Content: This is the world with the girl in it and this is the world with the boy in it.
Content_zh: 這是有女孩的世界，這是有男孩的世界。

Content: And these are the locations.
Content_zh: 而這些是地點。

Content: There's Sugar Bunting, her home town.
Content_zh: 這裡有 Sugar Bunting，她的家鄉。

Content: Meriloft, the Cloud Colony.
Content_zh: Meriloft，雲端聚落。

Content: Shellmound is there's some concept art in the back of a town made of sand castles.
Content_zh: Shellmound，後面有一些沙堡小鎮的概念圖。

Content: This is the Dead Eye God's pyramid.
Content_zh: 這是 Dead Eye God 的金字塔。

Content: This is the forest, from there.
Content_zh: 這是那邊的森林。

Content: Swisshelm: Games are complicated, man.
Content_zh: Swisshelm：遊戲很複雜。

Content: Like they're so complicated.
Content_zh: 真的超複雜。

Content: That's the one thing that this always, this drives me crazy.
Content_zh: 這就是一直讓我抓狂的一點。

Content: Even the most simple game, when you really break it down has many different elements that need to work together.
Content_zh: 就算是最簡單的遊戲，真的拆開來看，也有很多不同元素必須互相配合。

Content: And so, you know, Tim thinking about things on a high level and then also thinking about or actually writing dialogue,
Content_zh: 所以 Tim 一邊從高層次思考事情，一邊也會實際去寫對話，

Content: I think that's, that's a lot of, a lot of stuff to do, a lot of stuff to keep in your mind.
Content_zh: 我覺得那真的有很多事要做，也有很多事要記在腦子裡。

Content: So I think having, having, I think I was able to go in and help kind of be an external brain for him for a little while.
Content_zh: 所以我想我能進去幫他一陣子，像是替他當外接大腦。

Content: Just to think about some of the high level things that he hasn't had time to process in a while.
Content_zh: 幫他想一些他最近沒時間消化的高層次問題。

Content: So he could really focus on that dialogue that the team needs to use to, you know, get this section of the game up and running.
Content_zh: 這樣他就能專心寫團隊需要的對話，把遊戲這一段做起來。

Content: Schafer: So those are the kind of open design questions I'm working through.
Content_zh: Schafer：這些就是我正在處理的開放式設計問題。

Content: It's basically finish out the puzzle path for the girl.
Content_zh: 基本上就是把女孩這邊的謎題路線收完。

Content: Swisshelm: Designers have a big responsibility because other people's work depends on them.
Content_zh: Swisshelm：設計師責任很大，因為其他人的工作都得靠他們。

Content: What gets in the game and you know, what they do, what, if you make an assumption and then it ends up not working
Content_zh: 遊戲裡會放什麼、大家要做什麼；如果你做了一個假設，最後卻行不通，

Content: and you have to throw away a bunch of stuff, now you've just cost somebody, something that they've,
Content_zh: 就得丟掉一大堆東西，等於讓某個人投注心血的成果白費，

Content: they've put their heart and soul into; it might not be included in the game.
Content_zh: 那些東西可能就不會放進遊戲裡。

Content: And that's, and that's a reality, but it's also heartbreaking.
Content_zh: 那是現實，但也很讓人心碎。

Content: So I think it's a big pressure for game designers to make sure that they're using their tools to the best of ability
Content_zh: 所以我覺得遊戲設計師壓力很大，必須盡可能善用工具，

Content: to suss out some of those problems and work them out before it gets to the point where somebody is modeling or painting or animating.
Content_zh: 在有人開始建模、繪圖或做動畫之前，先找出那些問題並解決掉。

Content: Schafer: OK. Let's do the bucket puzzle.
Content_zh: Schafer：好，我們來做水桶謎題吧。

Content: How do you get the bucket from the hermit?
Content_zh: 要怎麼從隱士那裡拿到水桶？

Content: Swisshelm: It'd be nice if that worked together with the boars somehow.
Content_zh: Swisshelm：如果這能用某種方式跟野豬搭上就好了。

Content: Like maybe it's a feed bucket.
Content_zh: 也許是飼料桶。

Content: Schafer: It's a slop bucket.
Content_zh: Schafer：是餿水桶。

Content: Swisshelm: Yeah. And then you... Schafer: What?
Content_zh: Swisshelm：對，然後你……Schafer：什麼？

Content: Offscreen Voice: Milk.
Content_zh: 畫外音：牛奶。

Content: Schafer: Oh, he's milking the boars?
Content_zh: Schafer：喔，他在擠野豬奶？

Content: You like the boar milking?
Content_zh: 你喜歡擠野豬奶這點嗎？

Content: But he sells boars' milk.
Content_zh: 但他賣野豬奶。

Content: Later we'll have a puzzle that you use boars' milk.
Content_zh: 後面可以有個要用野豬奶的謎題。

Content: So how do you steal this poor guy's bucket?
Content_zh: 所以你要怎麼偷這個可憐人的水桶？

Content: Swisshelm: He could be using it in other ways.
Content_zh: Swisshelm：他可能用其他方式。

Content: He's catching a drip with it.
Content_zh: 他拿它接滴水。

Content: Schafer: It's his toilet.
Content_zh: Schafer：那是他的馬桶。

Content: It's his latrine.
Content_zh: 那是他的廁所。

Content: You have to get him really constipated so he doesn't need to use his latrine anymore.
Content_zh: 你得讓他嚴重便祕，這樣他就不用再用茅坑了。

Content: Swisshelm: Luckily, boars' milk stops you up.
Content_zh: Swisshelm：幸好野豬奶會讓人便祕。

Content: Schafer: Oh, yeah.
Content_zh: Schafer：是啊。

Content: Schafer: I mean, when we're thinking about, I guess I have a certain way of thinking about puzzles
Content_zh: Schafer：我是說，在思考謎題時，我大概有一套自己的想法。

Content: and certain elements that I always want them to have, like a certain, I try to have the, like the narrative of the story
Content_zh: 還有一些我一直希望它們具備的元素，比如我會希望故事敘事

Content: be something that would be actually fun to tell to someone else.
Content_zh: 要是講給別人聽也真的會有趣的東西。

Content: Like oh, I went here, I did this thing.
Content_zh: 像是：喔，我去了這裡，做了這件事。

Content: Like have the actual actions not sound really tedious, like.
Content_zh: 也就是讓實際要做的動作聽起來不要很無聊。

Content: And I put this moustache on a cat and you know.
Content_zh: 例如我把鬍子裝到貓身上之類。

Content: But have them sound like really fun, like I punched this sea monster in the mouth.
Content_zh: 而是讓它們聽起來很好玩，比如我朝海怪嘴裡揍了一拳。

Content: Schafer: Holy crap that's so productive.
Content_zh: Schafer：天啊，這也太有效率了。

Content: Oh, Paul didn't see, 'cause he was texting.
Content_zh: 喔，Paul 沒看到，因為他在傳簡訊。

Content: That's fine.
Content_zh: 沒關係。

Content: We totally did three puzzles today essentially.
Content_zh: 我們今天基本上完成了三個謎題。

Content: Awesome.
Content_zh: 太棒了。

Content: Swisshelm: Sweet.
Content_zh: Swisshelm：讚。

Content: Schafer: Our rule in the old days, always two puzzles a day.
Content_zh: Schafer：以前我們的規矩是每天兩個謎題。

Content: Swisshelm: OK.
Content_zh: Swisshelm：好。

Content: Schafer: So three puzzles we're pretty much taking the day off tomorrow.
Content_zh: Schafer：既然今天做了三個謎題，我們明天差不多可以休假了。

Content: Swisshelm: We only have to do one tomorrow, yeah. That's great.
Content_zh: Swisshelm：那我們明天只要做一個，對。太好了。

Content: Schafer: No, he had, he brainstormed with me and we talked about the puzzles for the remaining part of the game.
Content_zh: Schafer：不是，他跟我一起腦力激盪，我們討論了遊戲剩下部分的謎題。

Content: He had some good ideas and now I've got to wrap that up, but it's kind of undetermined right now,
Content_zh: 他有一些好點子，現在我得把它收尾，但目前還有點不確定。

Content: 'cause I need to know the scope that I'm designing to.
Content_zh: 因為我需要知道自己設計時的限制範圍。

Content: And that's what Matt Hanson is coming up with now.
Content_zh: 那就是 Matt Hanson 現在正在整理的東西。

Content: He's coming up with what he calls the currency.
Content_zh: 他正在整理他所謂的「貨幣」。

Content: So OK, you've got three cut scenes here.
Content_zh: 比如說，好，這裡有三段過場動畫。

Content: You can spend them here or you can spend them over here.
Content_zh: 你可以在這裡花，或者在這裡花。

Content: So I kind of need to know those constraints to, so I can wrap up the design.
Content_zh: 所以我得知道那些限制，才能把設計收尾。

Content: Hanson: We're kind of breaking the game down by scenes, so we're able to put a value, a cost value to each one of those scenes.
Content_zh: Hanson：我們把遊戲拆成各個場景，這樣就能替每個場景估一個成本值。

Content: And it gives us a better estimation of how long it's going to take to actually builds those things.
Content_zh: 這能讓我們更準確估計實際做出那些東西要花多久。

Content: And it's just going beyond what we had originally anticipated.
Content_zh: 這已經超出我們原本的預期。

Content: And that that was something that, we just were able to get to that realization by getting more insight into the design.
Content_zh: 我們也是因為更了解設計內容後，才意識到這一點。

Content: Petty: I don't know that the scope of the game is really growing as much as our understanding of it.
Content_zh: Petty：我不確定遊戲規模是真的變大，還是我們對它的理解變深了。

Content: It's a big game.
Content_zh: 這是款大遊戲。

Content: I mean, it's not a, it's not a small game just in terms of the scope of environments, characters, puzzles.
Content_zh: 我是說，光看環境、角色、謎題的規模，它就不是一款小遊戲。

Content: You know, it's not always a correlation to like play-through time, you know, like that's very variable, especially in adventure games.
Content_zh: 這不一定跟通關時間直接相關，尤其冒險遊戲的通關時間變動很大。

Content: But just in terms of the scope of the game, it's a pretty big game.
Content_zh: 但光看遊戲範圍，它就是款很大的遊戲。

Content: Schafer: I think one of our problems, though, or maybe one of my problems is just the ambition of the game.
Content_zh: Schafer：不過我覺得我們的問題之一，或者說我的問題之一，就是遊戲的野心。

Content: Like I always have a very ambitious games.
Content_zh: 我做的遊戲總是很有野心。

Content: And when we're doing like a $400,000 game, probably hadn't triggered that feeling yet in me.
Content_zh: 當我們還以 40 萬美元規模在做時，大概還沒觸發我那種感覺。

Content: I was thinking small.
Content_zh: 我在想小。

Content: But as soon as it got big, I was like OK, we're doing it.
Content_zh: 但它一變大，我就覺得，好，我們要認真做了。

Content: We're doing a big game.
Content_zh: 我們是在做一款大型遊戲。

Content: Schafer: The current schedule has us going out until other millenniums, which is going to be hard on us financially so we are... Petty: Generational game?
Content_zh: Schafer：照目前時程，我們會做到另一個千禧年，財務上會很吃力，所以我們……Petty：跨世代遊戲？

Content: Schafer: Yeah. It's something that I, I mean our kids' kids will finish it,
Content_zh: Schafer：對。就是我們孩子的孩子會完成它，

Content: but just knowing that they will have a thing of beauty to worship in forever will be worthwhile.
Content_zh: 但想到他們會有一件美麗的作品可以永遠崇拜，就值得了。

Content: Hansen: The game is going to cost more than we originally anticipated.
Content_zh: Hansen：這款遊戲的成本會比我們原先預期更高。

Content: Is that something that we can recoup at some other time on the project or from other projects or whatever,
Content_zh: 這筆錢能不能之後從這個專案，或從其他專案之類的地方補回來，

Content: it's, it's something I'm, luckily I don't have to deal with.
Content_zh: 幸好我不用處理。

Content: Bailey: There's actually very few avenues available to us.
Content_zh: Bailey：其實我們能走的路很少。

Content: We could take on outside investment, but when you look at as far as the schedule goes
Content_zh: 我們可以接受外部投資，但從時程來看，

Content: and how big the story is, like how we get material funds to finish this.
Content_zh: 再加上故事規模這麼大，要拿到足夠資金把它完成，

Content: And you know, it just gets to a point where potentially it's insurmountable.
Content_zh: 就可能變成跨不過去的難題。

Content: So, but that's why, you know, we have clever people all working together, all thinking about this and figuring out how we can make this happen.
Content_zh: 但也正因為這樣，我們才需要這些聰明人一起想辦法，看怎麼讓它成真。

Content: So you know, I could go and it'd be like, hey, we need another million.
Content_zh: 所以我可以去說，嘿，我們還需要一百萬。

Content: I can get a million without strings attached, got a million.
Content_zh: 如果我能弄到不附帶條件的一百萬，那就是一百萬到手。

Content: Schafer: Let's see how Brutal Legends sold.
Content_zh: Schafer：來看看《Brütal Legend》賣得怎麼樣。

Content: It said $916,593, dollars.
Oh, oh, yeah, yeah, dollars.
Content_zh: 喔，對，對，美元。

Content: That's awesome.
Content_zh: 太棒了。

Content: [Applause] Bailey: Got a million.
Content_zh: [掌聲] Bailey：拿到一百萬了。

Content: OK, we need another million.
Content_zh: 好吧，我們需要另一百萬。

Content: OK, I got another million for you.
Content_zh: 好吧，我還有一百萬給你。

Content: Schafer: We are launching the Humble Double Fine Bundle today.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們今天要推出 Humble Double Fine Bundle。

Content: And if it makes a ton of money, maybe we won't have to cut anything from the game.
Content_zh: 如果能賺很多錢，也許我們就不用從遊戲裡砍掉任何東西。

Content: It's just like the Kickstarter all over again.
Content_zh: 這根本像 Kickstarter 又重演一次。

Content: Bailey: It's just nice.
Content_zh: Bailey：就是感覺很好。

Content: Like I watch money going out every day.
Content_zh: 我每天都看著錢流出去。

Content: I love these events when we have; we actually have a chance to see some money coming in.
Content_zh: 我很喜歡這種活動，因為終於有機會看到錢流進來。

Content: Schafer: I really am not going to get my hopes up any higher than like $200,000.
Content_zh: Schafer：我真的不會把期待拉高到超過二十萬美元。

Content: I think Justin is hoping for like a million dollars.
Content_zh: 我想 Justin 期待的是一百萬美元之類。

Content: Bailey: OK, I got another million for you.
Content_zh: Bailey：好，我再幫你弄一百萬。

Content: OK, we need six million.
Content_zh: 好，我們需要六百萬。

Content: Schafer: We've had a too much game to make in too little time problem the whole time.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們一直都有「遊戲太大、時間太少」的問題。

Content: And there's just been periods we've been able to forget about it.
Content_zh: 只是有些時候我們可以暫時忘掉這件事。

Content: Like, I'm really happy about the trailer.
Content_zh: 像是，我真的很滿意那支預告。

Content: Let's just forget about the fact that we don't know how we're going to get the rest of the game done on time.
Content_zh: 就先忘記我們其實不知道怎麼準時完成剩下的遊戲吧。

Content: But it's still there.
Content_zh: 但問題還是在那裡。

Content: And I think now this is the most direct, head-on attack we've been making towards fixing the problem.
Content_zh: 我想現在這是我們最直接正面處理這個問題的一次。

Content: You know, the scheduling stuff that Matt's doing is really quantifying it to the point where we can attack it instead of just talking about attacking it.
Content_zh: Matt 做的時程規劃真的把它量化到我們可以處理，而不是只是在嘴上說要處理。

Content: 'Cause I've been thinking about it like when I'm designing like how to simplify stuff,
Content_zh: 因為我做設計時一直在想怎麼簡化東西，

Content: but it's obviously a much bigger, much more drastic actions.
Content_zh: 但這顯然需要更大、更激烈的行動。

Content: Schafer: Why don't you mention your crazy idea?
Content_zh: Schafer：你為什麼不提一下你的瘋狂主意？

Content: Bailey: OK. A crazy idea.
Content_zh: Bailey：好，一個瘋狂的想法。

Content: So we have, it's like, it's, so you're a writer, obviously.
Content_zh: 所以，你顯然是作家嘛。

Content: And when you sit down to write a story and you basically, you look at it, you think maybe you have like a book.
Content_zh: 當你坐下來寫故事，看著它，一開始可能覺得自己是在寫一本書。

Content: And then you maybe realize you have a novel.
Content_zh: 然後你可能發現，其實那是長篇小說。

Content: And then looking back, Schafer: What's the difference between a book and novel?
Content_zh: 然後回頭看……Schafer：書和小說差在哪？

Content: Bailey: Well, I think there'd be more pages.
Content_zh: Bailey：嗯，我想頁數會更多。

Content: So, and then you have like, you have a maybe, maybe you even have a series.
Content_zh: 再接著，可能甚至會變成一個系列。

Content: Like maybe that's the whole story to tell.
Content_zh: 也許那才是完整要講的故事。

Content: And the thought is, is that we potentially have scope-wise enough content for a game to ship sooner rather than later.
Content_zh: 想法是，以規模來看，我們可能已經有足夠內容可以先推出一款遊戲。

Content: So we're talking about acts and the stuff is actually broken up into acts and then maybe the Act One provides a great cliff-hanger
Content_zh: 所以我們在談幕數，而內容本來就分成不同幕，也許第一幕能提供很棒的懸念結尾，

Content: for potentially another installment, which would be Act Two and the conclusion.
Content_zh: 接著可能再推出另一部，也就是第二幕和結局。

Content: But that, that was the idea.
Content_zh: 這大概就是那個想法。

Content: I mean, I guess some reaction might be, oh, but you leave the story in a place where there's a lot of doubt,
Content_zh: 我猜有些人的反應可能會是：喔，可是你把故事停在充滿疑問的地方，

Content: but it's, you know, it's almost like The Empire Strikes Back, right?
Content_zh: 但這有點像《帝國大反擊》，對吧？

Content: Where you end it and you have all that anticipation building up.
Content_zh: 你在那裡結束，讓期待感累積起來。

Content: It also gives us a chance for, you know, if the game is going to cost six million right now,
Content_zh: 這也讓我們有機會，因為如果這款遊戲現在要花六百萬，

Content: if it costs three million, you know, that's basically, we've spent all the money that's come in for us by the backers.
Content_zh: 如果它花三百萬，那基本上就是把贊助者給我們的錢都投入進去了。

Content: So we've delivered on the mandate that we spent all the money to make a game of a very high quality.
Content_zh: 所以我們也算達成承諾：把所有錢都拿來做出一款非常高品質的遊戲。

Content: And then the second game, you know, from the sales of the first game, we can then fund the second game.
Content_zh: 然後第二款遊戲就能用第一款的銷售收入來資助。

Content: Which I think is actually pretty elegant, so.
Content_zh: 我覺得這其實很漂亮，所以……

Content: And it gives us a chance to reuse art, reuse the engine that we've already developed, you know,
Content_zh: 而且這讓我們有機會重用美術、重用已經開發好的引擎，

Content: it seems, seems like a pretty good solution.
Content_zh: 看起來是個不錯的解法。

Content: Stamos: I like the idea of delivering something.
Content_zh: Stamos：我喜歡先交出一些東西這個想法。

Content: Schafer: I mean, the assumption did come up once during Grim Fandango, because Grim was late.
Content_zh: Schafer：我是說，《Grim Fandango》時期也有人提過這個想法，因為 Grim 延期了。

Content: And people were like, it does, and then more than this, that story's split into four years really easily.
Content_zh: 大家會說，確實可以，而且不只如此，那個故事很容易拆成四個年份。

Content: Like let's do it in four episodes.
Content_zh: 像是，做成四集吧。

Content: But if we had done that based on the perception of the sales of that game, there never would have been the rest of Grim Fandango.
Content_zh: 但如果當時照那款遊戲的銷售觀感來做，就永遠不會有後面的《Grim Fandango》了。

Content: We never would have made it because the first one, Stamos: But it was a boxed product, too, right?
Content_zh: 我們根本做不下去，因為第一部……Stamos：但那也是盒裝產品，對吧？

Content: Schafer: Yeah. But we would have made the first one and they would have been like, well, that wasn't a huge hit.
Content_zh: Schafer：對。但我們做完第一部後，他們可能會說，嗯，這不是大賣。

Content: And so they would have just cut it.
Content_zh: 所以他們就會把它砍掉。

Content: And that's why I'm torn about it, 'cause I'm kind of glad we didn't do it then, 'cause it did get, it did get made.
Content_zh: 這也是我糾結的原因，因為我有點慶幸當時沒那樣做，畢竟它最後真的完成了。

Content: And you know, it made its numbers internally there.
Content_zh: 而且它在公司內部其實達到預期數字了。

Content: Rice: Wanted me to just, we all know how big the amount of hype is for this game, more so than anything that we've worked on since I've been here.
Content_zh: Rice：我們都知道這款遊戲的熱度有多高，比我來這裡之後做過的任何東西都高。

Content: And it seems like it'd be unfortunate to squander that.
Content_zh: 浪費掉這股熱度似乎很可惜。

Content: Bailey: In a weird, Schafer: Who's squandering it, though?
Content_zh: Bailey：某種奇怪的……Schafer：不過誰在浪費？

Content: Which part?
Content_zh: 哪部分？

Content: Rice: It just seems like since there's so much excitement and so much hype built up around it,
Content_zh: Rice：只是覺得既然它累積了這麼多期待和熱度，

Content: that means that there's going to be a lot of excitement when it comes out.
Content_zh: 那代表它推出時會有很多人興奮。

Content: And so we should try to deliver the best project that we can to capitalize on it.
Content_zh: 所以我們應該交出能把這股熱度發揮到最大的最佳作品。

Content: Bailey: But see, that's the interesting part, what you're talking about.
Content_zh: Bailey：但你看，你說的有趣之處就在這裡。

Content: Is because our main worry is that this gets cut short and we have to wrap it up quickly, right?
Content_zh: 因為我們主要擔心的是內容被砍短，然後必須很快收尾，對吧？

Content: And I think by not actually releasing something early and commercializing it, that actually becomes more of a risk than if we do.
Content_zh: 我覺得如果不先推出一部分並讓它開始營收，反而會比這麼做風險更高。

Content: Schafer: It's definitely in Justin's mind this is what's happening.
Content_zh: Schafer：Justin 心裡絕對覺得這就是會發生的事。

Content: Definitely in Greg's mind it's not happening.
Content_zh: Greg 心裡則絕對覺得這不會發生。

Content: And I'm really in the middle.
Content_zh: 而我真的在中間。

Content: Like I, I'm willing to do it because I know it's something we might not have any option not to do
Content_zh: 我願意這麼做，因為我知道這可能是我們不得不做的選項，

Content: and really to make, try and turn it into a positive thing.
Content_zh: 也想努力把它變成一件正面的事。

Content: But I also know there's going to be some problems associated with it.
Content_zh: 但我也知道它會帶來一些問題。

Content: And actually if we could trim back the schedule enough an stretch the money enough to where they meet and we can actually do it,
Content_zh: 其實如果我們能把時程縮到夠短、把錢撐到能接上，真的做得完，

Content: I'd probably save it and just release it all at once.
Content_zh: 我大概會把它留著，然後一次全部推出。

Content: Petty: In some ways it's letting us make it the length that it wants to be.
Content_zh: Petty：某種程度上，這讓我們能把它做成它該有的長度。

Content: By releasing something that is a compelling stop point, it's just not a complete product, but it's a compelling stop point.
Content_zh: 先推出一個有吸引力的停頓點，它不是完整產品，但會是一個有說服力的停頓點。

Content: And I think if we, my feeling is that if we told the backers that like, 'cause they keep saying how do we get to more money?
Content_zh: 我的感覺是，如果我們告訴贊助者，因為他們一直在問：我們要怎麼幫你們弄到更多錢？

Content: You know, if you look at the forums, they're like these are hardcore guys.
Content_zh: 你看論壇就知道，他們都是很核心的玩家。

Content: This is saying like releasing it, clearly saying its the first act, you guys get it for, I don't know, a week or whatever.
Content_zh: 也就是清楚告訴他們：我們會先推出第一幕，你們可以先玩，我不知道，也許先玩一週之類。

Content: Then we're going to start some online sales.
Content_zh: 然後我們會開始線上銷售。

Content: People will buy the product, they're not backers but they're going to get to actually purchase the product
Content_zh: 不是贊助者的人也會購買，真的把產品買下來，

Content: because we need that money to make the game that we want to, that you deserve.
Content_zh: 因為我們需要那筆錢，才能做出我們想做、也是你們應得的遊戲。

Content: Which is true.
Content_zh: 這是真的。

Content: It's not smoke up their ass.
Content_zh: 不是在唬爛他們。

Content: But I think it's just a funding model to be able to do that.
Content_zh: 但我覺得這只是讓我們能做到那件事的一種籌資模式。

Content: Schafer: 'Cause like this is like in some ways a sneak peek.
Content_zh: Schafer：因為這某種程度上就像搶先看。

Content: This is just like you're getting this half of it early.
Content_zh: 這只是讓你提早拿到前半部。

Content: Bailey: Yeah, we could make it, it's actually not the release, right?
Content_zh: Bailey：對，我們可以把它做成其實不是正式發行，對吧？

Content: This is not the release.
Content_zh: 這不是正式發行。

Content: This is just, this is for, Petty: It's a pre-release.
Content_zh: 這只是，這是給……Petty：這是預先發布。

Content: Rice: I think it's a good solution potentially to a way to get more money for this project,
Content_zh: Rice：我覺得這可能是個不錯的辦法，可以替這個專案多籌一點錢，

Content: but it isn't as good for the game necessarily.
Content_zh: 但對遊戲本身不一定那麼好。

Content: It's just a good way to fund the rest of the development.
Content_zh: 它只是替剩下的開發籌資的好方法。

Content: Bailey: You know, with Greg, this project is his baby.
Content_zh: Bailey：你知道，對 Greg 來說，這個專案就像他的孩子。

Content: It's Tim's baby, too.
Content_zh: 它也是 Tim 的心血。

Content: So like, I kind of defer to Tim on what he wants to do with it.
Content_zh: 所以我會尊重 Tim 想怎麼處理它。

Content: But it is sometimes hard to step outside that and take the riskier course when it's something you've been working so passionately towards.
Content_zh: 但當你一直很投入地朝某個目標努力時，有時候很難跳出來，選擇風險更高的路線。

Content: Rice: And that's kind of what we're trying to figure out now.
Content_zh: Rice：這就是我們現在在想的事。

Content: And I think that it's also, I have a unique perspective being that I'm the studio's brand manager
Content_zh: 我也覺得，身為工作室的品牌經理，我有一個比較特別的觀點，

Content: and so I also have to like responsible for everything outward facing that comes out of the studio
Content_zh: 所以我也得負責工作室所有對外發布的內容，

Content: and what people, what people's perception is of what we're doing.
Content_zh: 以及大家會怎麼看我們正在做的事。

Content: I feel like we've been in this cycle for four years of jumping from contract to contract and game to game
Content_zh: 我覺得這四年我們一直在這個循環裡，從一個合約跳到另一個合約，從一款遊戲跳到下一款。

Content: and we need to take a bet on a game that has the best chance of anything that we've worked on, of making us money and getting us out of that cycle.
Content_zh: 我們得賭在一款最有機會賺錢、讓我們脫離這個循環的遊戲上。

Content: Schafer: We could still do all that.
Content_zh: Schafer：我們還是可以做到那些。

Content: We could still put that money in, Rice: But I think it just cheapens the product.
Content_zh: 我們還是可以把那筆錢投入……Rice：但我覺得那會讓產品顯得廉價。

Content: It just feels like I'm not getting a full game.
Content_zh: 只是覺得我沒有得到一個完整的遊戲。

Content: I'm getting a game that wasn't the original vision.
Content_zh: 我拿到的是一款不符合原始願景的遊戲。

Content: Schafer: But you are getting it.
Content_zh: Schafer：但你還是有拿到它。

Content: You're just getting a look at it sooner.
Content_zh: 你只是更早看到它。

Content: You're getting a look at the first half sooner.
Content_zh: 你只是更早看到前半部。

Content: Bailey: I think we're kind of stuck on the traditional route.
Content_zh: Bailey：我覺得我們有點被傳統路線困住了。

Content: It's like it's, this is forging new territory.
Content_zh: 這是在開拓新的領域。

Content: I mean, I love this.
Content_zh: 我是說，我很喜歡這點。

Content: Rice: I don't think there's anything we've done that's traditional here.
Content_zh: Rice：我覺得我們在這裡做的事本來就不傳統。

Content: Bailey: Exactly. So why start now?
Content_zh: Bailey：沒錯。那為什麼現在才開始傳統？

Content: Schafer: I got the solution here, right there.
Content_zh: Schafer：我這兒有解法，就在那裡。

Content: This is the solution to all our problems.
Content_zh: 這就是解決我們所有問題的辦法。

Content: Yes.
Content_zh: 對。

Content: I cashed outed all the backer money; I'm leaving. Whoo.
Content_zh: 我把贊助者的錢都領出來了，我要走了。嗚呼。

Content: Tomorrow I'm going to go pick up the Barracuda; it's finally done.
Content_zh: 明天我要去牽 Barracuda，它終於修好了。

Content: And the mechanic is like, it needs to be cash.
Content_zh: 然後技師說，得付現金。

Content: You just don't ask questions.
Content_zh: 你就別問問題。

Content: It needs to be cash.
Content_zh: 得付現金。

Content: Just bring the cash.
Content_zh: 把現金帶來。

Content: But it's also twice what we originally talked about it costing.
Content_zh: 但費用也是我們一開始談的兩倍。

Content: Mechanic: The thing with yours is that it was hard to know where to stop, when you cut it open.
Content_zh: Mechanic：你這台的問題是，拆開之後很難知道該在哪裡停手。

Content: Where do you draw your lines?
Content_zh: 你要在哪裡劃線？

Content: Like, you know, the heater/air conditioner should have really come out while I was under there.
Content_zh: 像是我在底下作業時，其實應該把暖氣／冷氣總成拆下來。

Content: And that should have been fixed.
Content_zh: 那也應該修好。

Content: And then that means the air conditioning compressor should be fixed and plumbed and recompressed.
Content_zh: 那就表示冷氣壓縮機也該修好、接好管線，重新加壓。

Content: And the steering column.
Content_zh: 還有轉向柱。

Content: I took it out and I'd like to have changed the turn signal switch, but it was working.
Content_zh: 我把它拆下來，本來也想換方向燈開關，但它還能用。

Content: So I just put the column back in, you know.
Content_zh: 所以我就把轉向柱裝回去了。

Content: I didn't, I cleaned it and everything but I didn't take it apart.
Content_zh: 我沒有拆；我把它清乾淨了，但沒有拆開。

Content: So there are places you got to draw your lines where you stop on a project like yours.
Content_zh: 所以有些地方你必須劃清界線，決定專案做到哪裡就停手。

Content: Schafer: It's hard to know where to stop.
Content_zh: Schafer：很難知道該在哪裡停手。

Content: Schafer: So it's like, I mean, I went and looked at all the work.
Content_zh: Schafer：所以我是說，我去看了所有施工內容。

Content: It's not like he's like, he just did a lot of stuff that needed to be done.
Content_zh: 不是他亂做，他只是做了很多本來就該做的事。

Content: There's a new electrical harness on it and new starter.
Content_zh: 上面換了新的線束和新的啟動馬達。

Content: But he also like fixed all these switches and stuff.
Content_zh: 但他也把這些開關之類的東西都修好了。

Content: It's going to be much better and so it's really going to be a pleasure to drive.
Content_zh: 車況會好很多，開起來真的會很舒服。

Content: It just cost twice as much as I thought.
Content_zh: 只是花費是我原本以為的兩倍。

Content: Pretty much just like Broken Age.
Content_zh: 簡直就像《Broken Age》。

Content: Schafer: It was a journey that I went through, in a way.
Content_zh: Schafer：某種程度上，這是我經歷的一段心路歷程。

Content: Mentally, of like hating the idea and actually feeling this is the way to save the game.
Content_zh: 心理上從討厭這個點子，轉變到覺得這其實是拯救遊戲的方法。

Content: Schafer: I'm going to talk about the thing.
Content_zh: Schafer：我要談那件事了。

Content: There's a thing going on which we've been talking about.
Content_zh: 有件事我們一直在談。

Content: Would you like to hear about the thing?
Content_zh: 你想聽聽嗎？

Content: It's about an idea.
Content_zh: 重點是一個點子。

Content: A scheduling idea for this game, because we have, as you know, kind of a schedule problem, which is that I designed a game
Content_zh: 這是這款遊戲時程上的點子，因為大家也知道，我們有點時程問題，也就是我設計了一款遊戲，

Content: that's too big for the schedule that we have and the money that we got.
Content_zh: 以我們現有的時程和資金來說太大了。

Content: The other idea that was put forth recently was what about releasing Part One of the game early.
Content_zh: 最近有人提出另一個想法：要不要先推出遊戲第一部？

Content: And at first I was like, oh, that sounds terrible, because like the first half of the game ends in such a cliff-hanger and all this.
Content_zh: 一開始我覺得，喔，那聽起來很糟，因為遊戲前半段結束在很大的懸念上。

Content: But as an alternative to cutting the game content in half, and I do like that idea a lot better.
Content_zh: 但比起把遊戲內容砍半，我其實喜歡這個想法多了。

Content: Offscreen Voice: You think you might like piss people off?
Content_zh: 畫外音：你覺得這可能會惹惱大家嗎？

Content: Schafer: Depending on how we do it, it could definitely piss people off.
Content_zh: Schafer：要看我們怎麼做，但確實有可能惹惱大家。

Content: But yeah, if you're giving them a choice of hey, you guys, we could either cut the design in half
Content_zh: 可是，如果你給他們一個選擇：嘿，各位，我們可以把設計砍半，

Content: or we could just go out and try to find some money magically and then ship the game next year or you could get the first half now.
Content_zh: 要不然我們就出去神奇地找一筆錢，明年再推出遊戲；或者你們現在就能玩到前半部。

Content: And I think people, I don't know what they would say.
Content_zh: 大家會怎麼想，我也不知道。

Content: Like I would think people would want to, want to play what they could play.
Content_zh: 我會覺得大家應該會想先玩到能玩的部分。

Content: As long as it was done, finished and good and not rushed out the door, I think they would like to play what they could play.
Content_zh: 只要它有做完、品質夠好，不是倉促推出，我想他們會想玩到能玩的部分。

Content: Rice: I'm feeling more comfortable about it, so I mean, it's just a matter of you want the perfect game
Content_zh: Rice：我對這件事比較安心了。我的意思是，問題在於你想做出完美的遊戲，

Content: and we, you know, you're working so hard with a really distinct vision in mind.
Content_zh: 而且你一直很努力，心裡有非常清楚的願景。

Content: And so now we're at the point where it's like, OK, realities have set in.
Content_zh: 所以現在我們到了這個階段：好，現實已經擺在眼前。

Content: We got to ship this game at some point.
Content_zh: 我們總得在某個時間點推出這款遊戲。

Content: Ultimately like we're just doing what's best for the game.
Content_zh: 歸根究柢，我們只是想做對遊戲最好的事。

Content: We want the game to succeed.
Content_zh: 我們希望遊戲成功。

Content: We want it to be good.
Content_zh: 我們希望它好。

Content: We want people to be excited about it, but we also want to be sensitive to the needs of the studio.
Content_zh: 我們希望大家對它感到興奮，但也得顧到工作室的需求。

Content: And I think that there's a way that we can do all of those things.
Content_zh: 我想我們有辦法兼顧這些事。

Content: We ran into the same thing with Iron Brigade.
Content_zh: 我們做《Iron Brigade》時也遇過同樣的事。

Content: I remember we had a meeting where like our Microsoft producer flew down.
Content_zh: 我記得我們開過一場會，Microsoft 的製作人還飛過來。

Content: We sat in this room for three days and it was like, we might cancel your game unless you can prove to us that you can finish it in,
Content_zh: 我們在這個房間裡坐了三天，對方就像在說：除非你們能證明可以在期限內完成，不然我們可能會取消你們的遊戲。

Content: you know, however many months and make it good.
Content_zh: 不管還有幾個月，都要把它做好。

Content: And we had to like figure out how to change everything, all the structure, cut things and make really smart decisions on how to actually get it done.
Content_zh: 我們得想辦法調整所有東西、整個架構，刪內容，做出很聰明的決定，才能真的完成。

Content: And it was an awful meeting and everybody hated each other, but we came out of it with a really solid plan
Content_zh: 那場會議很糟，大家都看彼此不順眼，但最後我們拿出了一套很紮實的計畫，

Content: and we knew what we were going to do.
Content_zh: 也知道接下來該怎麼做。

Content: And I think this is just that stage where it's like the huge hump to get over, but at the end of this we're going to have like a clear plan
Content_zh: 我覺得現在就是那個階段，眼前有個很大的難關要跨過，但走完之後，我們會有清楚的計畫，

Content: for the rest of production.
Content_zh: 支撐接下來的製作。

Content: Everyone's going to know what needs to happen and we're going to just, you know, plow through it.
Content_zh: 每個人都會知道接下來該做什麼，然後我們就一路推進。

Content: [Music] Schafer: It feels like the right thing to do.
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：感覺這是正確的做法。

Content: But at least we have an actual, real schedule now that is not based on just our hopes and dreams
Content_zh: 但至少我們現在有一份真正可執行的時程，不只是靠希望和夢想。

Content: but actually has math that shows that we'll be done in a certain month.
Content_zh: 而是真的有數字顯示我們會在某個月份完成。

Content: January for Part One and April for Part Two.
Content_zh: 第一部一月，第二部四月。

Content: [Music] Schafer: Well, nothing really.
Content_zh: [音樂] Schafer：嗯，其實沒什麼。

Content: I mean I feel like, I feel like we want to show what making games is really like and this is what making games is really like.
Content_zh: 我是說，我覺得我們想呈現做遊戲真實的樣子，而這就是做遊戲真實的樣子。

Content: Like tough choices, tradeoffs, compromises, arguments, you know.
Content_zh: 像是艱難選擇、取捨、妥協、爭論。

Content: And then in the end it all turns out to be something great.
Content_zh: 最後一切都會變成很棒的成果。

Content: Hopefully.
Content_zh: 希望如此。

Content: Offscreen Voice: Part one of something great.
Content_zh: 畫外音：偉大作品的第一部。

Content: Schafer: Part one of something great.
Content_zh: Schafer：偉大作品的第一部。

Content: Yeah.
Content_zh: 對。
